tax: Placed at genus rank, Bloomling is defined by composition and formation mechanics, not by colour, size, or danger: a porous bog-iron body. A new species belongs here only if it shows a porous ferrous bog body.
ap: The grouping is recognised by a hunched rust-red spongy mound. A field surveyor confirms membership from at least one persistent marker — an orange rust-bleed and iron filings — which survives even when the body disperses.
eco: Bloomling occupies iron-stained Still-water bogs. Persistence is elemental rather than reproductive: members form where earth-pressure cements a bog-iron bloom and recur with the bog re-precipitates the bloom, sustaining themselves on earth-pressure in iron mud.
beh: Across the grouping, behaviour is the disturbance-response of the medium: lurches to ram and magnetically pin. Roles, moods, and summoning circumstances are recorded on the entries below, not treated as new taxa.
traitsText: Shared characters: porous magnetic body and pressure-sense. These are grouping-level traits only; specific resistances, abilities, and field signs belong to each species and species beneath this node.
abilities: Grouping-level abilities are the broad capacities implied by the shared body: mass-ram and magnetic tool-drag. Named, bounded powers are assigned to the species that actually possess them.
bg: In the Throat, Bloomling matters because turns iron bogs into disarming traps. It lets survey reports sort porous bog-iron forms from dense ore ones without collapsing distinct hazards into one vague label.
g: Bloomling is the genus-rank grouping of porous bog-iron elementals within the True Elementals. Its members share a porous magnetic bog-iron body that rams and strips tools, and divide below into species groups by iron porosity and bog strength. A rust-bleeding mass that drags iron to itself.
